data.title: Stanley Black & Decker 10-Q: $3.85B Revenue, $0.39 GAAP EPS
data.description:
Stanley Black & Decker reported quarterly net sales of $3,846.4 million, up 3% year-over-year, with GAAP diluted earnings per share of $0.39 and non‑GAAP diluted EPS of $0.80 after adjustments. Revenue growth was driven by pricing and foreign exchange benefits, while volume declined modestly. The company reported GAAP net earnings of $59.6 million and non‑GAAP net earnings of $122.2 million after pre-tax adjustments totaling $81.0 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $3,846.4 million, +3% YoY (price +3%, FX +3%, volume -3%).
  • Gross Profit: $1,157.3 million; gross margin 30.1% of sales (adjusted 30.2%).
  • Earnings before income taxes: $84.8 million (impacted by $81.0 million pre-tax non‑GAAP adjustments).
  • Net Earnings (GAAP): $59.6 million.
  • Net Earnings (Non‑GAAP): $122.2 million after adjustments.
  • Diluted EPS (GAAP): $0.39 per share.
  • Diluted EPS (Non‑GAAP): $0.80 per share (adjusted).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and segment performance: Net sales growth was led by Engineered Fastening (+10%) while Tools & Outdoor increased 2%.
  • Channel and geographic trends: Tools & Outdoor experienced lower North America volume but stronger international sell‑in and professional conversions.
  • Brand momentum: Pricing actions and professional conversions supported core brands including DEWALT and CRAFTSMAN.
  • Portfolio and operational actions: Completed the CAM divestiture and executed supply‑chain footprint actions and restructuring aimed at reducing costs.
  • Cost and productivity programs: Completed a Global Cost Reduction program with approximately $2.1 billion run‑rate savings and is pursuing further footprint rationalization.

Original SEC Filing: STANLEY BLACK & DECKER, INC. [ SWK ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Constellium Q1: Revenue $2,461M, EPS $1.42 — Strong YoY Profit Recovery
data.description:
Constellium reported first-quarter revenue of $2,461 million and diluted earnings per share of $1.42, driven by higher revenue per ton amid slightly lower shipments. The company delivered a marked improvement in profitability with net income rising to $196 million from $38 million a year earlier. Management cited metal price effects on revenue and positive operating cash flow despite working capital impacts from higher inventories and receivables.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $2,461 million, up 24% year-over-year.
  • Gross Profit: $337 million (as implied by disclosed figures).
  • Operating Income: $254 million (as implied by disclosed figures).
  • Net Income: $196 million, improved from $38 million in the prior year.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Share: $1.42 attributable to equity holders of Constellium.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Mix: Packaging & Automotive Rolled Products (P&ARP) represented 60% of revenue and 58% of Adjusted EBITDA, reflecting robust demand in packaging and automotive rolled products.
  • Segment Momentum: Aerospace & Transportation shipments rose 18% year-over-year, supported by demand in aerospace, transportation and defense and constrained North American supply.
  • Operations & Capacity: Continued investments in recycling and casting capabilities; the company operates 24 manufacturing sites and 3 R&D centers with approximately 11,500 employees.
  • Working Capital & Cash Flow: Higher metal prices elevated inventories and receivables, while operations still generated positive operating cash flow for the quarter.

Original SEC Filing: CONSTELLIUM SE [ CSTM ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: SOL price drops towards $83 as Solana ETF flows dwindle
data.description:
Solana’s native token, Solana (SOL), traded near $83 on Wednesday after failing to hold above the $85 mark, extending its decline as investor momentum cooled and exchange-traded fund (ETF) inflows slowed.

At the time of writing, SOL was trading at around $83.40, posting a modest 0.4% gain over the past 24 hours.

Despite the slight daily increase, the broader trend remains under pressure.

The token has fallen 5.7% over the past seven days and remains down more than 43% over the previous year.

Weak ETF demand adds pressure to price action

Solana’s recent decline has coincided with fading institutional enthusiasm tied to spot crypto investment products.

Earlier optimism surrounding Solana-focused ETF products had supported stronger buying activity, helping SOL maintain higher trading ranges.

However, recent data suggests those inflows have moderated significantly, removing an important source of upward pressure.

Institutional participation remains a key factor for major digital assets in 2026, particularly as firms such as BlackRock, Fidelity, and Grayscale continue to shape broader crypto market sentiment through regulated investment products.

While Solana remains a prominent altcoin in institutional discussions, weaker ETF-related momentum has reduced buying urgency in recent sessions.

This shift has left SOL more vulnerable to technical breakdowns, especially as broader crypto markets remain cautious amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

Technical indicators point to growing downside risk

SOL’s rejection below $88 has emerged as a key technical development.

Price charts now show repeated lower highs—a structure often associated with increasing seller control.

Momentum indicators such as the Relative Strength Index (RSI) have also softened, pointing to weakening buyer strength.

Immediate support is forming near $80, a level traders are closely monitoring.

A break below this threshold could expose SOL to further downside toward the $75–$70 range.

Some projections also identify $63–$67 as a stronger medium-term support zone if bearish pressure intensifies.

Trading volume remains elevated

Despite weakening momentum, trading activity in Solana remains substantial.

SOL recorded more than $3.74 billion in 24-hour trading volume, reflecting active participation from both short-term traders and longer-term investors adjusting positions.

High trading volume during declining price action often indicates increased defensive positioning, with market participants reacting to uncertain signals rather than strong conviction buying.

This suggests traders are closely watching near-term support levels while waiting for clearer catalysts.

Long-term fundamentals remain intact

Although technical indicators currently favour caution, Solana’s broader ecosystem remains relevant.

The blockchain continues to rank among the largest smart contract platforms, with ongoing adoption across decentralised finance, NFTs, and payment infrastructure.

Companies such as Visa have previously integrated Solana for stablecoin settlement initiatives, while developers continue building on its high-speed network.

SOL also remains significantly above its historical low of $0.5008 recorded in May 2020, though it is still roughly 71.5% below its all-time high of $293.31 reached in January 2025.

This contrast highlights the gap between Solana’s long-term growth trajectory and its current technical correction.

data.title: Analysts See 79 Billion-Cubic-Foot Rise in U.S. Natural Gas Inventories
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

U.S. natural gas inventories likely saw an above-average increase last week as mild spring weather limited demand, according to a survey of analysts by The Wall Street Journal.

Natural gas stored in underground facilities is expected to have risen by 79 billion cubic feet to 2,142 Bcf in the week ended April 24, according to the average estimate of 11 analysts, brokers and traders. Estimates range from an injection of 72 Bcf to an injection of 84 Bcf.

The expected storage build is larger than the five-year average increase for the week of 63 Bcf, and would put inventories 153 Bcf above the average, compared with a 137 Bcf surplus the week before.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration is scheduled to release the storage data on Thursday at 10:30 a.m. EDT.

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: Knight-Swift Transportation Holdings — Q revenue $1.85B, EPS $(0.01)
data.description:
Knight-Swift Transportation Holdings reported quarterly revenue of $1,850.2 million and diluted EPS of $(0.01), reflecting modest top-line growth while profitability was pressured. Operating income fell sharply year-over-year, driven by higher insurance and accounts-receivable securitization costs, resulting in a slight net loss attributable to the company. Management noted continued cash generation with positive operating cash flow and free cash flow for the quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $1,850.2 million, quarterly change +1.4% versus prior year.
  • Operating income: $28.6 million, decreased 57.1% year-over-year; MD&A cites higher insurance/claims and AR securitization costs as drivers of the decline.
  • Net (loss) income: $(1.4) million, attributable to Knight‑Swift $(1.3) million, a swing from $30.6 million in the prior year period.
  • Diluted net income per share: $(0.01), versus $0.19 in the prior year.
  • Cash flow: Net cash provided by operating activities $142.5 million; Free Cash Flow $56.9 million, with MD&A noting solid cash generation.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and trends: Consolidated revenue modestly increased year-over-year while revenue excluding fuel was essentially flat, indicating mixed volume and yield dynamics across segments.
  • Channel and brand momentum: Strength noted in truckload yield and intermodal load growth. Logistics revenue declined but revenue per load improved. LTL business showed improvements in mix and weight.
  • Operational efficiency: Truckload utilization and miles per tractor improved; Truckload Adjusted Operating Ratio was modestly worse but management emphasized operating discipline and ongoing integration progress at U.S. Xpress.
  • Capacity and network expansion: Continued build-out of the LTL network (now 180 facilities) with a slower facility pace planned for 2026. Intermodal partnerships and warehousing growth are expanding the company’s service breadth.

Original SEC Filing: Knight-Swift Transportation Holdings Inc. [ KNX ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026

data.title: Thursday preview: BoE, ECB policy announcements due
data.description:
In the UK, Endeavour will publish first-quarter results, along with Standard Chartered, while Glencore will put out a Q1 production report.

Trading updates are due from Inchcape, Lancashire, Persimmon, Magnum Ice Cream and Unilever, along with FY results from Premier Inn owner Whitbread.

Whitbread shares surged on Monday following a report it’s planning to sell a swathe of hotels to unlock £1.5bn for shareholders. According to The Times, the company is expected to announce plans alongside its results for the sale and leaseback of one in five of the freehold hotel properties it currently owns outright, following a strategic review of its business model.

As far as policy announcements are concerned, the BoE and ECB are expected to stand pat on rates at 3.75% and 2.0%, respectively.

Danske Bank said it’s pencilling in no rate changes for the coming 12 months when it comes to the BoE, but with risks tilted towards hikes.

"Data has come in mostly on the hawkish side since March as the economy has weathered the energy blow better than expected," it said. "Like consumer sentiment, it could still turn sour, though."

It added: "Hiking rates will have to be weighed against a considerable risk of exacerbating a looming economic contraction. We think it is most likely the BoE will remain sidelined for the foreseeable future."

Danske also expects the ECB to leave the deposit rate unchanged, in line with consensus and market pricing. It expects Lagarde to leave the door open for summer hikes in order to keep inflation expectations anchored, but at the same time not pre-commit to hikes.

"We expect the ECB to increase policy rates by 25 basis points in June and July," Danske said.

Regarding Apple’s results, Kathleen Brooks, research director at XTB, said it won’t just be revenues that investors want to hear about.

"We have already heard that Tim Cook is stepping down in September and John Ternus will succeed him. The company is expected to report revenues of $109.45bn for last quarter, but investors may want to get some sense of what Ternus will bring to Apple when he takes over later this year. Will he push Apple down the AI route, something Cook was unwilling to do?

"Apple is also known for its shareholder sweeteners, and share buybacks and dividends could also be on the cards. This may boost enthusiasm for the stock, which is basically flat year to date."

Also in the spotlight on Thursday will be first-quarter US GDP figures, amid expectations for a 2.3% increase, and for core prices to have jumped 4.1% from 2.7% in Q4 2025.

Kathleen Brooks said: "However, after Wednesday’s raft of economic data from the US, there are some who are concerned that the growth rate could disappoint expectations after weakness in housing starts and building permits in February.

"A weaker than expected GDP reading, or a stronger than expected rise in the prices index, could knock confidence in financial markets, which is already roiled by events in the Middle East."

data.title: DCC surges on takeover proposal from Energy Capital, KKR
data.description:
"The board of DCC is evaluating the proposal together with its advisers and a further announcement will be made in due course," it said.

It added that there can be no certainty that any firm offer will be made, nor as to the terms on which any firm offer might be made.

Under UK takeover rules, the consortium has until 1700 BST on 10 June to either announce a firm intention to make an offer or walk away.

The shares closed up 10.1% at 5,924.21p.

Danni Hewson, head of financial analysis at AJ Bell, said: "DCC made itself an easier entity to swallow by hiving off its health and technology units to focus on the sale, marketing and distribution of energy.

"The undisclosed cash offer from a private equity consortium comes at a time when DCC is likely to benefit from current elevated energy prices and shareholders will want the company to leverage this advantageous backdrop in any takeover discussions."
